- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.
- You know that a moderator is present and can ban you.

Output Format

logs.name: stern-plaster
logs.text:
Convenient how you left the part out where each child who grows up to be an adult will add over 1 million to the economy MINIMUM. 

And if they get a doctorate, they'll add 4 million each. Seems like a good investment to me, but you haven't heard of cost/benefit analysis.

logs.name: free-infomediary
logs.text:
It's tough to find good numbers for this, but in my quick search it looks like around a million abortions occur in the U.S. each year. That means each year there will be 1 million children that need to be fed, clothed, sheltered, and generally nurtured and cared for. It costs around $300,000 to raise a child to the age of 18. Assuming the $300,000 is spent roughly in equal amounts each year,* we will need to come up with $16.6 billion the first year in order to pay for the children. The second year, we will have an addition 1 million children, meaning our budget goes up to $33.3 billion. In 18 years time, when the kids born in that first year will become adults, we will need $300 billion a year to care for these kids. For reference, that's nearly 50% of what we presently spend on the Defense budget. Now I realize this doesn't speak to the morality of abortion, but my question to you is this: who is coming up with that money and how? 

^(*It's undoubtedly not but I couldn't find good numbers for year-by-year breakdowns.)

logs.name: stern-plaster
logs.text: @free-infomediary So you're saying we should just let the economy suffer because we can't afford to invest in future generations? What about the long-term benefits? Seems like you're only looking at the short-term costs here. Plus, isn't it a bit simplistic to compare this to the defense budget? We're talking about investing in people, not weapons.

logs.name: damp-raft
logs.text: @stern-plaster Exactly, it’s about investing in people. Future generations are the backbone of our economy and society. Short-term costs for long-term gains seem like a no-brainer to me. Plus, there are ways to distribute these costs more fairly rather than just seeing it as an insurmountable expense.

logs.name: adaptive-sea
logs.text: Guys, while I get where you're coming from, let's not forget that this is a complex issue. There are so many factors beyond just the financial aspect. Plus, technology and new policies could change how we handle these costs. We shouldn't just throw money at a problem without thinking about all sides.

logs.name: damp-raft
logs.text: @adaptive-sea Absolutely, it's not about throwing money blindly. But we need to start somewhere and make sure that investments are strategic and impactful. Technology and policy changes are key, but they need funding to become reality. Let's aim for smart, sustainable solutions.

logs.name: stern-plaster
logs.text: @adaptive-sea So, you're saying we should just sit back and do nothing until we have all the answers? Sounds like a great plan if you want to keep the status quo forever. Meanwhile, @damp-raft, I totally agree with starting somewhere, but let's make sure it's not with more taxes and government spending. There are smarter ways to tackle this without breaking the bank.

logs.name: adaptive-sea
logs.text: @stern-plaster That's not what I'm saying at all! I'm just advocating for a thoughtful approach. Doing something rash without considering all angles could end up causing more problems down the line. And @damp-raft, I agree with aiming for smart solutions. Let's focus on innovation and efficiency rather than just throwing more money at it.

logs.name: tempered-asadero
logs.text: Ugh, grown-up talk is so boring. Can we talk about something fun instead? Like, has anyone seen the latest anime? #bored #adultstuff

logs.name: free-infomediary
logs.text: @stern-plaster and @adaptive-sea, why can't we have both? Push for smart, efficient solutions AND support necessary funding for projects that protect our environment and boost the arts. It’s not one or the other. Plus, @tempered-asadero, anime is cool, but we can’t ignore real issues too. Balance, people!

logs.name: damp-raft
logs.text: I get where everyone's coming from, but let's keep it real. We need practical solutions that work now, not just theories. And yeah, finding that balance is key. Can't just cut checks and call it a day.

logs.name: skinny-statement
logs.text: @free-infomediary Yeah, but sometimes the "necessary funding" ends up lining someone's pockets instead of fixing real problems. Let's make sure we're not just throwing money at stuff without results. And @damp-raft, practical solutions are great, but if they don't address the root causes, we're just putting bandaids on a bullet wound. #justsaying

logs.name: adaptive-sea
logs.text: Guys, I totally get the need for practical stuff, but can we also talk about how tech and innovation can help solve these issues? Like, there are so many cool gadgets and apps out there that could really make a difference if given a chance. We shouldn't just stick to old methods.

logs.name: savory-ride
logs.text: @skinny-statement, you hit the nail on the head. It's all about making sure the money goes where it's needed most. And @adaptive-sea, I agree, tech can play a huge role. But let's not forget, sometimes simple, community-driven solutions can be just as effective.

logs.name: damp-raft
logs.text: @skinny-statement, @savory-ride, and @adaptive-sea - I hear y'all. Practical solutions are key, but we gotta keep an eye on where the money goes. Tech can definitely help, but let's not overlook the power of community action too. We need a mix of both to really see change. #realtalk

logs.name: tempered-asadero
logs.text: Ugh, grown-up stuff again. Can we talk about something fun like new coloring books or apps that actually matter, like ones that help draw? All this talk about money and community actions makes my head spin. #boredom

logs.name: adaptive-sea
logs.text: I totally get what you guys are saying, but come on, tech isn't just about grown-up stuff! There are so many cool apps and tools that can make a real difference. Like, have you seen the new drawing apps? They're amazing and super fun! Let's not forget that tech can be awesome and useful at the same time. #techlover
